title: Visit to the University of Liverpool and Symposium Participation
content:
Visit to the University of Liverpool and Symposium Participation

Our Head of Department, Dr. Ilknur SIMSEK, attended the Neurodiversity 2026 Symposium organized by the Faculty of Health and Life Sciences at the University of Liverpool and visited the University as part of the program. This year’s Neurodiversity 2026 Symposium stood out as a comprehensive scientific event that brought together academicians, researchers, practitioners, and individuals with neurodivergent profiles. TEDx speaker Shaun FLORES also took part in the event as one of the panelists.

The panels held throughout the Symposium covered a wide range of subjects, including: Diverse voices and lived experiences, Needs of parents and caregivers of neurodivergent children, Participation and support mechanisms, Mental health and neurodiversity, Creating accessible spaces and reasonable accommodations, Partnership approaches in reshaping local neurodevelopmental services and Communication with autistic individuals in primary healthcare.

Throughout the Symposium, Dr. SIMSEK followed current research in the field of neurodiversity, Engaged in discussions aimed at developing new academic collaborations, Had the opportunity to observe the scientific infrastructure and research facilities of the faculty on site. This visit represents an important step toward strengthening our Department’s international academic relations and expanding opportunities for future collaborative projects.

This content was updated on 10/04/2026.
